metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students explore choreographic structures, composition principles, and movement vocabulary to create and perform original dances. This course develops creative expression through the design and execution of dance pieces that communicate ideas and emotions. Students learn to work both independently and collaboratively while developing their artistic voice.
metadata.general_description: Create and perform original choreographed dances using composition principles and diverse movement vocabularies.

big_ideas.statement: Choreography is a process of intentional creative decision-making that transforms movement into structured artistic expression.
big_ideas.elaboration: Choreographers make deliberate choices about movement, space, time, and relationships to create meaningful dance works that communicate ideas and emotions to audiences.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding composition principles and movement vocabularies expands the possibilities for creative expression.
big_ideas.elaboration: Knowledge of themes, motifs, contrast, repetition, levels, directions, and various movement styles provides tools for choreographers to realize their artistic vision.

big_ideas.statement: The development of an original choreographic work requires experimentation, revision, and refinement.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through iterative processes of creating, testing, and refining movement ideas, choreographers develop works that effectively communicate their artistic intent.

big_ideas.statement: Performance and presentation of choreography is an essential part of the creative process.
big_ideas.elaboration: Sharing choreographed works with audiences allows for communication of artistic ideas and enables feedback that informs future creative development.

competencies.raw: Develop and express choreographic ideas using movement vocabularies
i_can_statements: I can experiment with different movement qualities, levels, directions, and pathways to explore choreographic possibilities
I can generate movement ideas using various compositional strategies such as improvisation, observation, and imagination
I can apply principles of rhythm, tempo, and musicality to choreographed movement
I can create movement that effectively communicates an artistic intention or theme

competencies.raw: Create original choreographic works using composition principles
i_can_statements: I can structure movement using compositional devices such as repetition, contrast, variation, and canon
I can organize movements into phrases, sequences, and sections to create coherent dance pieces
I can make intentional choices about space, formation, and spatial relationships in choreography
I can revise and refine choreographic ideas to strengthen artistic expression

competencies.raw: Use technology and other resources to support choreographic development
i_can_statements: I can use video recording to document, analyze, and refine choreographic work
I can apply music or sound design to enhance the impact of choreography
I can incorporate props, costumes, or lighting to support choreographic expression
I can research and learn from choreographic works by various artists to inform my own creation

competencies.raw: Experiment with movement from diverse cultural and contemporary dance traditions
i_can_statements: I can explore movement vocabularies from multiple dance styles and cultural traditions
I can respectfully incorporate cultural dance forms into my choreographic work
I can blend diverse movement styles to create unique artistic expressions
I can acknowledge and credit the origins of movement vocabularies used in my choreography

competencies.raw: Analyze choreographic choices and their effects on artistic meaning
i_can_statements: I can identify and explain how specific choreographic choices create meaning and communicate themes
I can analyze how compositional principles such as repetition, contrast, and variation affect audience understanding
I can consider how movement quality, timing, and spatial relationships influence the impact of choreography
I can articulate the relationship between artistic intent and choreographic decisions

competencies.raw: Evaluate choreographic work using established criteria and personal judgment
i_can_statements: I can assess choreographic work using criteria such as originality, technical execution, and artistic expression
I can identify strengths and areas for improvement in my own choreographic creations
I can provide constructive feedback to peers about their choreographic work
I can reflect on my growth as a choreographer and set goals for future development

competencies.raw: Connect choreographic choices to cultural contexts and personal experiences
i_can_statements: I can explore how cultural traditions and values are expressed through dance movement
I can draw on personal experiences and perspectives to inform choreographic creation
I can recognize how societal issues and themes can be explored through dance
I can reflect on the role of the choreographer as a storyteller and communicator

competencies.raw: Develop artistic judgment about choreographic effectiveness and innovation
i_can_statements: I can distinguish between imitation and creative interpretation in choreography
I can evaluate how well choreography fulfills its artistic purpose and communicates its theme
I can identify innovative approaches to movement and composition in choreographic works
I can make informed decisions about when to follow established conventions and when to break them

competencies.raw: Document choreographic work using various methods and notations
i_can_statements: I can record choreography using video documentation or digital notation systems
I can create choreographic notes that capture movement, counts, and transitions
I can use imagery and metaphor to describe choreographic intent and movement quality
I can organize documentation in ways that allow for reproduction and revision of choreographic work

competencies.raw: Perform choreographed work with focus, expression, and technical skill
i_can_statements: I can execute choreographed movement with accuracy, clarity, and appropriate quality
I can maintain focus and connection with fellow performers during choreographed work
I can express the artistic intent and emotion of choreography through my performance
I can adjust performance based on feedback and self-reflection

competencies.raw: Share choreographic work and artistic vision with audiences
i_can_statements: I can present choreographed work in formal or informal performance settings
I can articulate the artistic intent, inspiration, and development process of my choreography
I can engage audiences through performance presence and technical execution
I can respond thoughtfully to audience feedback and critical response

competencies.raw: Communicate through movement and choreographic choices about meaningful themes
i_can_statements: I can create choreography that explores personal, social, or cultural themes
I can use movement to express abstract concepts, emotions, or narratives
I can design choreography that invites audience interpretation and reflection
I can articulate how my choreographic choices contribute to the overall message or meaning

competencies.raw: Connect choreography to other disciplines and forms of artistic expression
i_can_statements: I can integrate music, visual art, or theatre elements into choreographic work
I can explore how choreography reflects or responds to literature, history, or social issues
I can collaborate with artists from other disciplines to create interdisciplinary works
I can recognize how choreographic principles apply to movement in other contexts

competencies.raw: Study and learn from professional choreographers and diverse dance traditions
i_can_statements: I can analyze choreographic works by established and emerging choreographers
I can identify stylistic elements and signature techniques of various choreographers
I can research dance traditions from diverse cultures and time periods
I can incorporate insights from professional choreography into my own artistic development

competencies.raw: Explore choreography as a career or ongoing artistic practice
i_can_statements: I can research career paths in choreography and professional dance contexts
I can develop a portfolio of choreographic work that demonstrates my artistic vision
I can engage with opportunities for mentorship and feedback from choreographic professionals
I can commit to ongoing development of my choreographic practice and artistic voice

competencies.raw: Share knowledge and inspire others through choreographic leadership
i_can_statements: I can teach movement ideas and choreographic concepts to peers
I can mentor younger or less experienced dancers in choreographic development
I can facilitate creative processes that support others' choreographic expression
I can contribute to building a community that values choreographic artistry and expression

areas.name: Movement Vocabularies and Technique
topics: Body awareness, alignment, and safe movement practices
Movement qualities: weight, flow, time, and space
Locomotor and non-locomotor movements in various styles
Levels, directions, pathways, and spatial patterns
Balance, coordination, and control in movement execution

areas.name: Choreographic Composition and Structure
topics: Compositional devices: repetition, variation, contrast, inversion, canon, and accumulation
Form structures: ABA, theme and variations, narrative, and abstract structures
Movement phrases and sequences in choreographic organization
Use of rhythm, tempo, and musicality in choreography
Spatial composition: formations, levels, and relationships between dancers
Creating coherence and artistic impact through structural choices

areas.name: Choreographic Process and Creative Development
topics: Improvisation techniques for generating movement ideas
Collaborative choreographic processes and ensemble creation
Documentation and notation of choreographic work
Revision, refinement, and technical development of choreography
Integration of music, sound, and other design elements
Problem-solving in choreographic development

areas.name: Dance Traditions, Contexts, and Artistic Expression
areas.raw: Dance Traditions, Contexts, and Artistic Expression
topics: Contemporary and classical ballet approaches to choreography
Jazz, hip-hop, and street dance styles and their choreographic applications
Cultural and indigenous dance traditions and choreographic contexts
Storytelling, narrative, and thematic expression through choreography
Choreography as artistic voice: personal expression and innovation
Performance presentation, stage presence, and audience engagement
